name: Ed Barlow
tagline: Software engineer, database architect, and published author. Building thoughtful systems for complex problems.
home_html:
Recent White Papers

  • Spec Driven Design Methodology ↗Best-practice specification-driven SDLC — overview and the full paper set.
  • How to Iterate in Spec Driven Design ↗How to change the spec and target so you can reproduce — four tries and my choice.
  • Your Agile Team — SDD Build Model ↗For small token-count work — an agile workflow for uncertainty management; you are Product Owner.
  • The Marina Framework ↗My big SDD implementation — a trust and sharing architecture on AWS.
  • My Workstation Setup ↗My workstation and how I work on projects — WSL, Docker, tooling.
  • Drydock ↗Specification-driven software delivery system — typed specs, build plans, evidence, and the Console.

Project Drydock

Drydock is a Full SDLC Specification Driven Development Framework that has built a ~200K token specification reliably using Sonnet. Drydock is in active development - I have laid the keel!

Testimonials

Drydock is a genuine superset of Spec Kit. Every Spec Kit concept maps cleanly to a Drydock equivalent: research lives in spikes with compiled evidence visible in the QuarterDeck, task breakdown and clarification are handled interactively by the QuarterDeck as a first-class artifact viewer, governance is covered by the RulesEngine across all projects, and Drydock is multi-agent by design. Drydock adds governed build execution, the QuarterDeck, the iterate loop, and documentation generation — none of which have Spec Kit equivalents. The superset claim is solid.

— Anthropic/Claude

Drydock is designed as a superset of GitHub Spec Kit: it preserves the core specification-driven lifecycle, maps each major Spec Kit concept into a Drydock equivalent, and extends the model with typed multi-file specifications, dependency-driven execution, governed rules propagation, evidence-backed review through the QuarterDeck, brownfield decomposition, documentation generation, and a specification-first iteration loop. The honest caveat is not conceptual weakness but product maturity: Spec Kit is the more established and field-proven implementation today, while Drydock is the broader architecture still being completed. The claim, therefore, is that Drydock is not yet the more mature product, but it is the more comprehensive delivery model, built to retain Spec Kit compatibility while carrying specification-driven development all the way through execution, review, and governed lifecycle management.

— OpenAI/Codex

Project Marina

The Marina is a Trust and Interoperability platform into which you can "dock" your projects. Process Based Methodology and scale-proven Technology Rules combine to "conform" your projects so they expose/encapsulate capabilities and enable an Enterprise Command Center.

  • Project Capabilities CatalogServices, Data, Shared Resources.
  • Agents enforce standardsYour Stack best practices, Your branding, and Automatic security.
  • Conformed DocumentationConsistent docs across all your projects.
  • Enterprise Command CenterLogging, Monitoring & Alerting, Scheduling, Pipelines.
  • AWS IntegrationSingle Library For AWS Service Integration - Built on their ultra low cost services.
